page: Microsoft Azure E128-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz, featuring 16 physical cores and 32 dedicated vCPUs with 2 threads per core. The hardware configuration includes 1024.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 32.0 GB of RAM per core. Additionally, the server is equipped with 7559 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, with zero bundled GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both ranking in the top 10%. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU extended instructions, demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and database operations fall into the average category. For machine learning workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 390.7 tokens per second on small LLM models, but performance drops to a poor rating of 2.44 tokens per second on large 70B models due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E128-32ads_v7 offers high memory density and substantial local storage capacity, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the added cost of GPU hardware. The server is well-suited for large-scale databases, memory-cached applications, data decompression pipelines, and static web serving. It is less optimal for heavy multi-core parallel processing or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E128-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs, 1024.0 GB of RAM, and 7559 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server delivers top-tier single-core performance and strong memory bandwidth, though multi-core scaling and large-scale LLM inference are more moderate. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core, it is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, large databases, and data decompression pipelines that do not require GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E128-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 32 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-capacity local storage and top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive database workloads.

Equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, offering 32.0 GB of memory per core
